title: Causal Masking Math, Conceptually
concept: Setting future similarity scores to negative infinity forces their softmax weights to exactly zero, blocking information leakage.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of using causal masking in AI?

options.text: To speed up training by skipping words
To prevent the model from seeing future words
To translate words into other languages

exercises.explain: Causal masking prevents the model from seeing future words, ensuring it only learns to predict the next word using past context.

cards.title: No Peeking!
cards.body: How do AI models look only at past words without accidentally 'peeking' at the future? They use a clever math trick that turns future possibilities into absolute zeros!

cards.title: Zeroing Out the Future
cards.body: Causal masking uses a mathematical trick: we replace the similarity scores of future words with negative infinity (-∞). When we apply the softmax function, these negative infinity scores turn into exactly 0% attention, successfully blocking any future data leakage.

cards.title: The Diary Blocker
cards.body: Imagine writing a diary entry but wearing glasses that blur out all pages ahead of today. You can read your past entries to understand your feelings, but you cannot peak into tomorrow because the future pages are completely blacked out.

cards.title: Masking in Action
cards.body: If we are predicting the next word in 'The cat sat on the...', the similarity score for the future word 'mat' is set to -∞. When the model calculates weights, 'mat' gets a weight of 0.0, ensuring the model only uses 'The', 'cat', 'sat', 'on', and 'the'.

cards.title: Myth: Future Words are Deleted
cards.body: People often think future words are just deleted or ignored by the model's code. In reality, the words are still in the matrix, but their mathematical scores are forced to negative infinity, which naturally drops their influence to zero.

cards.title: The Masking Shift
cards.body: See how changing the raw score of a future word to negative infinity affects the final attention weight.

exercises.prompt: What mathematical value is assigned to future similarity scores to block them?
exercises.explain: Using negative infinity (-∞) ensures that when the softmax function is calculated, the resulting attention weight becomes exactly zero.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Turning a future score into negative infinity results in a softmax attention weight of zero.
exercises.explain: Applying softmax to negative infinity results in an attention weight of exactly zero, which is how we block future information.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to show what happens to future weights.
exercises.sentence: Causal masking turns future attention weights to exactly _____ to prevent leakage.
exercises.explain: Setting scores to negative infinity makes their softmax weights zero, which blocks the AI from looking ahead.

exercises.prompt: Which developer has a flawed understanding of how causal masking math affects weights?

options.text: Developer A says: 'Future tokens are assigned negative infinity scores so their softmax weight becomes 0.0, blocking them.'
Developer B says: 'Future tokens are assigned negative infinity scores so their softmax weight becomes 1.0, making them dominant.'
Developer C says: 'Future tokens get a weight of 0.0 because of the negative infinity math trick.'

exercises.explain: The AI generated answer incorrectly claims that future tokens get a weight of 1.0, which would mean the AI only looks at the future! The human correctly states they get a weight of 0.0.

exercises.prompt: Why do AI models mathematically set future similarity scores to negative infinity (-∞) during causal masking?

options.text: It scales them by a factor of 10 to make them stand out.
It forces their softmax weights to zero, blocking future information leakage.
It averages them with past scores to smooth out the transition.
It turns them into positive infinity so the model focuses only on the future.

exercises.explain: Setting future similarity scores to negative infinity (-∞) makes their exponent in the softmax calculation exactly zero. This completely blocks information from future tokens, ensuring the model only pays attention to past and present tokens.
